DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata presentation rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api best-practice content design-specifications guideline interaction motion principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Variants Guideline

Documents all dimensions of visual or behavioral variation on a component. Each item in the variants array is either a flag variant (a boolean toggle such as 'disabled' or 'full-width') or an enum variant (a set of mutually exclusive options such as size: sm | md | lg). Every variant carries a purpose field explaining why the variation exists in the design system.

Source: guidelines/variant.schema.json

4 definitions in this file:

  • variantValue
  • flagVariant
  • enumVariant
  • variants

variantValue

A single option within an enum variant dimension.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable value name (e.g., 'sm', 'md', 'lg', 'primary', 'secondary').
descriptionrichTextrequiredWhat this value looks like, when to use it, and how it differs from the other values in this dimension.
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name (e.g., 'Small', 'Medium', 'Large', 'Primary', 'Secondary').
exampleexample[]optionalVisual previews showing this variant value in isolation or in context.
useCasesuseCase[]optionalScenario-driven guidance describing when this artifact is or is not the right choice.

References: richText, example, useCase

Example

[
  {
    "name": "primary",
    "displayName": "Primary",
    "description": "High-emphasis — the main action on the surface. Uses a solid, filled background. Limit to one primary button per surface.",
    "useCases": [
      {
        "description": "When the action is the most important on the surface — the one the user is most likely to take (e.g., Save, Submit, Confirm).",
        "kind": "positive"
      },
      {
        "description": "When a surface already has a primary button. Adding a second dilutes visual hierarchy.",
        "kind": "negative",
        "alternative": {
          "name": "secondary",
          "rationale": "Secondary emphasis maintains importance without competing with the existing primary action."
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "secondary",
    "displayName": "Secondary",
    "description": "Medium-emphasis — important but not the primary action. Uses a visible border and transparent background."
  },
  {
    "name": "ghost",
    "displayName": "Ghost",
    "description": "Low-emphasis — for tertiary actions, toolbar actions, or dense layouts where visual weight must be minimized."
  },
  {
    "name": "danger",
    "displayName": "Danger",
    "description": "Signals a destructive or irreversible action. Use for delete, remove, or disconnect actions.",
    "useCases": [
      {
        "description": "When the action is destructive or irreversible — deleting a record, revoking access, removing a team member.",
        "kind": "positive"
      },
      {
        "description": "When the action is not destructive, even if it feels important or urgent.",
        "kind": "negative",
        "alternative": {
          "name": "primary",
          "rationale": "The danger color is a strong signal reserved for destruction. Using it for non-destructive actions dilutes its meaning."
        }
      }
    ]
  }
]

flagVariant

A boolean/toggle variant that is either on or off. Use for binary capabilities such as 'disabled', 'full-width', 'icon-only', or 'loading'. Flag variants have no values array — the component either has the flag active or it does not.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"flag"requiredIdentifies this variant as a boolean flag.
namestringrequiredMachine-readable name of the flag (e.g., 'disabled', 'full-width', 'icon-only', 'loading').
descriptionrichTextrequiredWhat this flag controls and how it affects the component's appearance or behavior when active.
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name of the flag (e.g., 'Disabled', 'Full Width', 'Icon Only', 'Loading').
purposerichTextoptionalWhy this flag exists in the design system — the user need or design rationale it addresses (e.g., 'Prevents user interaction during form submission to avoid duplicate requests').
exampleexampleoptionalVisual previews showing the component with this flag active.
useCasesuseCase[]optionalScenario-driven guidance describing when this artifact is or is not the right choice.

References: richText, example, useCase

Example

[
  {
    "type": "flag",
    "name": "full-width",
    "displayName": "Full Width",
    "description": "When active, the button stretches to fill the full width of its parent container. Label text is centered.",
    "purpose": "Provides a prominent, easy-to-tap call to action in narrow or single-column layouts where the button should span the full container width.",
    "useCases": [
      {
        "description": "When the button is the sole action in a narrow container such as a mobile sheet, a card footer, or a single-column form.",
        "kind": "positive"
      },
      {
        "description": "When multiple buttons appear side by side in a button group or dialog footer.",
        "kind": "negative",
        "alternative": {
          "name": "default width",
          "rationale": "Full-width buttons in a horizontal group force a stacked layout, which breaks the expected left-to-right reading order for action groups."
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "type": "flag",
    "name": "icon-only",
    "displayName": "Icon Only",
    "description": "When active, the button renders a single icon with no visible label. An aria-label is required.",
    "purpose": "Enables compact, space-efficient actions in toolbars and dense layouts where the icon alone is universally understood."
  }
]

enumVariant

An enumerated variant with multiple mutually exclusive values. Use for dimensions of variation where the user selects one option from a set, such as 'size' (sm | md | lg), 'emphasis' (primary | secondary | ghost), or 'shape' (rounded | pill | square).

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"enum"requiredIdentifies this variant as an enumerated set of values.
namestringrequiredMachine-readable name of the variant dimension (e.g., 'size', 'emphasis', 'shape'). This is the axis name, not a value within the axis.
descriptionrichTextrequiredWhat this dimension of variation controls and how its values affect the component's appearance or behavior.
valuesvariantValue[]requiredThe possible values for this variant. Must contain at least two entries — a single-value dimension should be modeled as a flag variant instead. The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD preserve it for display.
Min items: 2
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name of the variant dimension (e.g., 'Size', 'Emphasis', 'Shape').

References: richText, variantValue

Example

[
  {
    "type": "enum",
    "name": "emphasis",
    "displayName": "Emphasis",
    "description": "Controls the visual weight of the button. Determines background fill, border treatment, and text color to establish a visual hierarchy among actions on a surface.",
    "values": [
      {
        "name": "primary",
        "description": "High-emphasis — the main action on the surface."
      },
      {
        "name": "secondary",
        "description": "Medium-emphasis — important but not primary."
      },
      {
        "name": "ghost",
        "description": "Low-emphasis — tertiary actions and dense layouts."
      },
      {
        "name": "danger",
        "description": "Signals a destructive or irreversible action."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "type": "enum",
    "name": "size",
    "displayName": "Size",
    "description": "Controls the physical dimensions and internal padding of the button. All sizes maintain a minimum 44×44 CSS pixel tap target.",
    "values": [
      {
        "name": "small",
        "description": "Compact size for toolbars, table rows, and dense layouts."
      },
      {
        "name": "medium",
        "description": "The default size. Suitable for most contexts including forms, dialogs, and page-level actions."
      },
      {
        "name": "large",
        "description": "Touch-optimized size for mobile-first surfaces and marketing pages."
      }
    ]
  }
]

variants

Documents all dimensions of visual or behavioral variation on a component. Each item is either a flag variant (a boolean toggle like 'disabled' or 'full-width') or an enum variant (a set of mutually exclusive options like size: sm | md | lg). Multiple items document orthogonal dimensions that can be combined independently.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"variants"requiredIdentifies this guideline as a variants specification.
itemsflagVariant | enumVariant[]requiredThe variant dimensions. Each item is either a flag (boolean toggle) or an enum (set of values). The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD display dimensions in this order.
Min items: 1

References: flagVariant, enumVariant

Example

{
  "type": "variants",
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "enum",
      "name": "emphasis",
      "displayName": "Emphasis",
      "description": "Controls the visual weight of the button to establish a visual hierarchy among actions on a surface.",
      "values": [
        {
          "name": "primary",
          "description": "High-emphasis — the main action on the surface."
        },
        {
          "name": "secondary",
          "description": "Medium-emphasis — important but not primary."
        },
        {
          "name": "ghost",
          "description": "Low-emphasis — tertiary actions and dense layouts."
        },
        {
          "name": "danger",
          "description": "Signals a destructive or irreversible action."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "enum",
      "name": "size",
      "displayName": "Size",
      "description": "Controls the physical dimensions and internal padding of the button.",
      "values": [
        {
          "name": "sm",
          "displayName": "Small",
          "description": "Compact size for toolbars, table rows, and dense layouts. 32px height."
        },
        {
          "name": "md",
          "displayName": "Medium",
          "description": "Default size for most contexts. 40px height."
        },
        {
          "name": "lg",
          "displayName": "Large",
          "description": "Touch-optimized size for mobile-first surfaces. 48px height."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "flag",
      "name": "full-width",
      "displayName": "Full Width",
      "description": "The button stretches to fill the full width of its parent container.",
      "purpose": "Provides a prominent call to action in narrow or single-column layouts."
    },
    {
      "type": "flag",
      "name": "icon-only",
      "displayName": "Icon Only",
      "description": "The button renders a single icon with no visible label. An aria-label is required.",
      "purpose": "Enables compact actions in toolbars and dense layouts where the icon alone is universally understood."
    }
  ]
}
↑ Back to top
